nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated, benthic; inhabits coral reefs and rocky shorelines at depths of 0–35 m

decorPreferences: live rock with caves and crevices

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on fish, molluscs, and crustaceans in the wild. In captivity, has been reported to accept squid, octopus, and meaty marine foods; live feeder fish may be needed initially to stimulate a feeding response. Generally fed every 2–4 days to satiation. Feeding with tongs or a feeding stick is generally advised to avoid accidental bites.

avoidWith: fish small enough to be swallowed

summary: The Fire Coral Eel (Gymnothorax miliaris) is a striking dark-bodied moray studded with golden spots, found across Atlantic reefs. Generally considered an intermediate-level species due to its escape tendencies and predatory appetite; has been reported to reach around 60–70 cm in well-kept aquariums.
careNotes:
The Fire Coral Eel is a medium-to-large moray eel that tends to spend much of its time concealed among live rock, extending its head from a crevice or ledge. It is widely considered an escape specialist — a tightly-fitted, gap-free lid is generally regarded as essential. Over time, individuals have been known to become less reclusive and rest visibly in the open.

Temperament: LiveAquaria's standard product page lists this species as semi-aggressive; the Golden Banana Moray page (same species, yellow morph) lists it as aggressive. Sources that focus on its reef-tank impact (LiveRock N Reef, FishIndex) describe it as a predatory eel that will consume anything it can fit in its mouth. Semi-aggressive has been filed here as the averaged designation, with the caveat that husbandry experience suggests aggressive behavior toward smaller tankmates.

Reef safety: LiveAquaria designates it 'with caution'; other sources consider it outright unsafe for invertebrate-heavy reef tanks. 'With caution' is the more common designation across aquarium trade sources, but keepers should be aware that crustaceans, shrimp, and small fish are generally considered at risk.

Water parameters were averaged across LiveAquaria (72–78°F / 1.020–1.025 SG), TankFacts (76–80°F / 1.023–1.026 SG), and FishBase environmental data (24.6–28°C). Resulting averages: temperature 23.7–26.8°C, salinity 1.022–1.026 SG.

Minimum tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 125 gal (473 L), FishIndex recommends 140 gal (530 L), and ReefApp recommends 800 L. Average is ~601 L; the 800 L outlier from ReefApp may reflect the full wild adult size of 70 cm. A larger tank is generally advisable.

Adult size: FishBase and ReefApp record a maximum of 70 cm; LiveAquaria cites 24 inches (61 cm). Averaged maximum is ~67 cm.

The 'Golden Banana Moray Eel' is a rare, fully yellow color morph of the same species (Gymnothorax miliaris) and commands premium pricing. The typical wild-type form is dark brown/black with scattered golden spots; color may vary. The 'Banana Eel' trade name is sometimes used for both the yellow morph and unrelated species — confirm scientific name before purchase.

This species is oviparous (egg-laying); captive breeding has not been documented in the aquarium hobby literature reviewed. It tends to be a solitary species in the wild; conspecific aggression has been reported when multiple individuals are housed together. Strong biological filtration and a protein skimmer are generally advisable given its high-protein diet and resultant waste load.
breedingNotes: Captive breeding has not been documented for this species in the aquarium hobby. In the wild, Gymnothorax miliaris is oviparous. Reproductive behavior in home aquaria is generally considered impractical.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary product page for the Fire Coral Eel (standard dark morph). A second LiveAquaria page for the Golden Banana Moray Eel (yellow morph, same species) was also consulted at https://www.liveaquaria.com/product/1423/?pcatid=1423.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Muraenidae / Anguilliformes), native range (Western + Eastern Atlantic), max size (70 cm), and temperature data (24.6–28°C). Also confirmed IUCN: Least Concern.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Provided additional water parameter values: 76–80°F, SG 1.023–1.026, dKH 8–12, pH 7.8–8.4. Used in averaging temperature, salinity, and pH.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Confirmed 70 cm max size; recommended minimum tank of 800 L (outlier vs. LiveAquaria 473 L and FishIndex 530 L; all three averaged to ~601 L). Noted reef-safe-with-caution designation.

additional.name: FishIndex
additional.notes: Provided additional tank size reference (140 gal / 530 L); confirmed carnivore diet and escape-risk behavior. Listed as reef-unsafe; contributed to reef-safe disagreement noted in careNotes.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive breeding has not been documented. The species is oviparous in the wild.
